question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to a valid account ID format but one that maps to a high-value or system account (e.g., '0.0.800'), causing the network node to perform additional authorization checks or trigger special-case processing paths for each submitted transaction, increasing its CPU consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to '10000' simultaneously, so that the pinger both floods the network node with transactions at near-nanosecond intervals and exhausts the mirror node with HTTP retries, creating a combined resource amplification that exceeds 30% above baseline?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '1ns' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to '2147483647', causing the backoff() function in transfer.go to compute near-zero sleep durations for all retry attempts and drive billions of sequential CryptoTransfer submissions against the network node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '876000h' (100 years) so that each HTTP connection to the mirror node holds a server-side goroutine and socket open indefinitely, and with HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES set high, accumulates thousands of open connections that exhaust the mirror node's file descriptors and increase its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a duration string with a unit not recognized by time.ParseDuration (e.g., '1d') for HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF, causing toDuration() to silently return 1*time.Second, and then exploit the predictable 1s backoff combined with a high maxRetries to generate a sustained retry storm against the network node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with duplicate NodeAccountID values across many nodeEntry records, causing the network map in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to be overwritten repeatedly and the SDK to establish redundant gRPC connections to the same node, increasing that node's connection-handling resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoint entries having Port=50211 (the standard Hiero gRPC port) and IPAddressV4 pointing to the network node, so that the SDK routes all transactions to that single node and concentrates its resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to the maximum int64 value (9223372036854775807) so that each CryptoTransfer transaction requires the network node to validate a transfer amount that may trigger overflow checks or special large-value processing paths, increasing per-transaction CPU cost and overall node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a very large value, so that the network node must process high-frequency, high-value transactions that trigger additional ledger consistency checks, increasing its resource consumption by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that performs an HTTP redirect to an internal mirror node endpoint, causing the http.Client in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to follow the redirect and expose internal service endpoints, then use the resulting network map to direct all transactions to a specific node and increase its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_BASE_BACKOFF to '1ns' so that the exponential backoff computation `cfg.mirrorNodeClientBaseBackoff * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))` in mirror_node_client.go produces near-zero delays for all retry attempts, causing rapid-fire HTTP requests to the mirror node and increasing its CPU and connection-handling resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns' and observe that the time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) in main.go will fire as fast as the Go runtime allows, effectively removing the rate-limiting intent of the interval parameter and causing the network node to receive transactions at the maximum rate the pinger can generate, increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a value that causes the attempts variable in submitWithRetry() (computed as cfg.maxRetries + 1) to overflow int, wrapping to a negative number and causing an infinite or very long retry loop that continuously submits transactions to the network node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with nodeEntry records having empty NodeAccountID fields (which are skipped) mixed with valid entries, causing the pinger to build a sparse network map that routes all transactions to fewer nodes, concentrating resource consumption on those nodes above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a nodesEnvelope with a single nodeEntry pointing to the target network node, so that the Hiero SDK sends all CryptoTransfer transactions exclusively to that one node rather than distributing them across the network, increasing that node's resource consumption by over 30%?
